headline: Departure of Ukraine's popular defense minister sparks protests
description: Rallies in support of Mykhailo Fedorov, Ukraine's young and popular defense minister, were held in Kyiv and around the country on Thursday, after President Volodymyr Zelensky angered politicians, military officials and the general public by announcing his removal the previous day.

headline: Morocco's frenzy for purchasing surveillance tools revealed by whistleblower
Pegasus Project: Five years after revelations about a global phone surveillance system, judicial investigation continues
As her deputy questions promise to ban headscarves, Le Pen doubles down
description: Jordan Bardella has expressed doubts about one of the Rassemblement National's key proposals. But Marine Le Pen is considering putting it to a referendum, if she is elected.

headline: Trump seeks to take control of US elections
description: The US president is pushing his 'SAVE America Act,' a bill that would tighten voting requirements and could penalize low-income voters and minorities.

headline: Heatwave-battered France adopts carbon neutrality roadmap for 2050, but must prove its credibility
description: On Wednesday, the Environment Ministry unveiled the country's third national low-carbon strategy, the official document charting the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions through the year 2050. The goals are highly ambitious compared to previous policies.

headline: 'Mad Max' on the ocean: The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
description: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.
byline: Liselotte Mas,  Elsa Delmas and  Thomas Saintourens

description: France fell hard to Spain on Tuesday in the semifinals of the football tournament, marking a cruel and unexpected ending for Didier Deschamps' squad, who had delighted supporters since the start of the competition.

headline: For Macron, the legalization of aid in dying meant a long personal journey
description: After doubting the need for new legislation, fearing he might upset religious leaders and concerned about dividing French society, the president eventually resolved to support the legislation, under pressure from MPs and public opinion.

headline: Why France's national weather service struggles to accurately predict heatwaves
description: Days ahead of the heatwaves of June and July, Météo-France slightly underestimated their intensity and duration. The reason has to do with limits in forecasting models for extreme events, as well as the challenge of explaining the uncertainty of weather predictions to the general public.
byline: Audrey Garric and  Raphaëlle Besse Desmoulières
